texto: In our spreading materialist wasteland, more and more people thirst for contemplation, including political wheel-dealers, tycoons, and military top brass, stressed out by the rigors of their professions. Are we to suppose that everyone doing deep breathing on a meditation pillow is communing with the God of Christians? Not necessarily. What unites us to God is the practice of love. If prayer, or any other religious act, is not grounded in that, it is an offense to God.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Dean Brackley que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “In our spreading materialist wasteland, more and more people thirst for contemplation, including …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, contemplation, meditation, com foco em meditation e social-justice.
